body.title: Consequence check: Web UI four-pane device tabs
body.result: Device-tab layout shipped but section order wrong (Announce/Health/Controls/Chat vs. Status/Chat/Feed/Settings); TTL aging not visible; startup health poll not confirmed. File T-web-ui-section-reorder as follow-on.
body.intention: I intend that the four-section device-tab layout (Announce/Health/Controls/Chat) renders and functions per the D-web-ui-controls design spec.
body.description:
**What changed:** D-web-ui-controls (2026-06-01) specified per-device tabs with four sections each: Announce (channel feed), Health/Status (key=value pairs from /health endpoint with TTL aging), Controls (circuit breakers), Chat. T-web-ui-three-pane and T-web-ui-circuit-breakers were marked closed 2026-06-04, but live UI shows only a flat Public feed.

**Predicted unintended effects:** device tabs don't render (JS logic missing or not wired), health surface polling not connected, TTL aging not implemented, /health endpoint exists but not called by web server.

**Signals to watch:**
- Opening http://localhost:8080 or equivalent: does the UI show device tabs (Igor/Librarian/Granny) or just a Public feed?
- Does opening an Igor tab show four sub-sections visually distinct?
- Do posted key=value status messages appear in the Health section and age out after TTL?
- Does CC.0 circuit breaker toggle kill cc-T-* tmux sessions within 5s?

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py (device-tab JS rendering, /api/device/list, /api/device/{id}/events); no new files
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** IN: verify four-section layout renders, health surface populates and ages, circuit breakers gate dispatch. OUT: custom styling, performance optimization.
**Completion criteria:** Open web UI, verify device tabs are clickable and show four sections. Post a test key=value status message to Igor's status channel, verify it appears in Health section. Open Igor's circuit breaker, verify cc-T-* session is killed and Granny posts GRANNY_THROTTLED on next dispatch.
**Test plan:** No new tests — this is live UI verification. Record behavior in ticket result field.
